content: Added `UIShadow.Enabled`, allowing `UIShadow`s to be individually toggled.

When deleting or renaming attributes in the Generator module of a ProceduralModel, Studio will automatically remove the old attributes from the ProceduralModel to avoid stale attributes accumulating as you edit.

The `export` keyword has been extended to support variables and functions. This works by constructing an export table and returning it from the module with the respective fields set, providing a new, consistent way for users to expose a stable API from aÂ module.
Adds a visual debugger for Instance stitching to the Server Authority debugger.
Improves terrain handling.
Adds a read only Studio UI property `IsInSandbox` to sandbox-able instances. This property represents if the given instance or its ancestors has sandboxing enabled.

`Class.AvatarEditorService:PromptCreateOutfit` now accepts two new optional parameters (`outfitOptions` and `outfitType`) that let you create Makeup outfits in addition to regular Avatar outfits. When outfitType is omitted, the engine infers the correct type from the HumanoidDescription contents.
Adds "Segment By Part" beta feature.
Improve error messages for Sandboxing & Capabilities interactions

Fixes animation graph bug where a sequence or select node (`Enum.AnimationNodeType.SequenceNode`, `Enum.AnimationNodeType.PrioritySelect`, `Enum.AnimationNodeType.RandomSequenceNode`, `Enum.AnimationNodeType.Select`) do not advance with a 0-duration clip.
Fixes a bug with `Class.CurveAnimation`, where adding a marker on the last keyframe of a curve didn't fire.
Fixes thumbstick directional keycodes in the Input Action System to apply Response Curve and Deadzone calculations properly.

Fixes an issue causing some properties of the following types to have incorrect default values when created in Studio: `Sound`, `BloomEffect`, `UIGridLayout`, `UIListLayout`, `UIPageLayout`, `UITableLayout`, `UIShadow`.
Fixes an issue where `Class.AudioListener` would flip its front/back channels in surround-sound formats.
In the New Luau Type Solver, type inference of function statements on tables are now more tolerant to generic functions.
Fixes an issue where `TextLabel` with auto-scaled rich text may disappear under small scales.
Fixes SLIM rendering visible from inside large buildings.
Fixes an issue where a client could fail to create a tool weld after re-equipping.
Fixes an issue where rejoining voice chat could bypass the Audio API (e.g. `Class.AudioDeviceInput`).
Output Window now stacks prints that include links to Instances (e.g. "â–¶ Baseplate (x2)").
Adds a pop-up when a user is kicked from a play test that mirrors the message a player receives in-game.
Animation Graph Editor: Fixed crash when graph contains a clip you don't have permissions for
